title: Fuel and emission control structure description - On-board snapshot

blocks.text: Fuel and emission control structure description - On-board snapshot

To facilitate fault diagnosis in the event of engine stalls and starting failures, a dedicated on-board snapshot is used to record the operating conditions of the PGM-FI ECU in the event of engine stalls and starting failures.
There are three types of on-board snapshots: on-board snapshot during DTC storage, on-board snapshot during engine stall, and on-board snapshot during startup failure.
Each of the on-board snapshot data stored by the PGM-FI ECU can be verified by using HDS.
Since only the first on-board snapshot can be stored in the PGM-FI ECU, the on-board snapshot must be cleared after data confirmation.

rows.text: On-board snapshot when DTC is stored
Stores vehicle status at the time of input/output component failure (DTC store)
Memorize vehicle status at the time of engine stall
Memorize vehicle status at the time of startup failure

blocks.text: The prerequisites for recording an on-board snapshot during engine stall are as follows: If an engine stall occurs under the prerequisites, the parameters related to the engine stall for a total of 10 seconds (9 seconds before the engine stall and 1 second after the engine stall) will be stored in the PGM-FI ECU. FI ECU.

items.text: After 5 seconds have elapsed after engine start (engine speed 500 rpm or more) (If the engine stalls within 5 seconds after engine start, it is stored in the on-board snapshot (complete explosion stall) side at the time of start failure.)
Engine Engine speed is less than 3,000 rpm when stall occurs
1 D/C after resetting the PGM-FI ECU or removing the battery terminal
Not in the middle of a SCS short

blocks.text: Three types of on-board snapshots are set for poor startup: long startup time, no startup, and complete explosion stall. The prerequisites for the recording of the on-board snapshot during a start failure are as follows: If an engine stall occurs under the prerequisites, the parameters related to the engine stall are stored in the PGM-FI ECU.

items.text: 1 D/C after resetting the PGM-FI ECU or removing the battery terminal
Not in the middle of a SCS short

blocks.text: The start failure judgment timer is variable depending on the engine cooling water temperature at engine startup, and the relationship between engine cooling water temperature and engine startup time is as follows.

rows.text: At engine startup Engine coolant temperature (°C)
Engine start time (sec) (Timer to determine starting failure)

blocks.text: Long start-up time and no start-up

In the case of long starting time and no starting, in the starting failure on-board snapshot, if cranking (engine speed 500 rpm or less) is longer than the specified time (starting failure determination timer: varies depending on the conditions at startup) and a starting failure is determined, the parameters related to the starting failure for a total of 10 seconds (9 seconds before the starting failure determination and 1 second after the starting failure determination) are stored in the PGM-FI ECU. The PGM-FI ECU stores the parameters related to the starting failure for a total of 10 seconds (9 seconds before the starting failure judgment and 1 second after the starting failure judgment).

In the on-board snapshot during a start failure at the time of a complete engine stall, despite the engine starting (engine speed of 500 rpm or more) within the specified time (start failure determination timer: varies depending on the conditions at startup) for cranking (engine speed of 500 rpm or less), within 5 seconds after engine startup. If an engine stall occurs, the PGM-FI ECU stores the parameters related to the complete engine stall for a total of 10 seconds (9 seconds before the engine stall and 1 second after the engine stall).
